diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: alpha1 anti-trypsin deficiency, TORCHES-CLAP infections, hepatitis (A, B, and C), anti-Ro/anti-LA antibodies
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: Diagnosis was made after other causes of neonatal liver failure were ruled out, including alpha1 anti-trypsin deficiency, TORCHES-CLAP infections, hepatitis (A, B, and C), and anti-Ro/anti-LA antibodies. The presence of severe neonatal liver failure of intrauterine onset associated with extrahepatic siderosis that spares the reticuloendothelial elements supported the diagnosis of neonatal hemochromatosis.
